# Pre-Implementation Review Checklist: Class Dynamics Engine

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality across all spec artifacts before coding begins. Tests whether requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and cover all necessary scenarios.
**Created**: 2026-02-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [tasks.md](../tasks.md)
**Depth**: Standard (~30 items)
**Audience**: Reviewer (pre-implementation gate)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the weights for the composite dispossession risk calculation explicitly specified? FR-003 states "each component weighted by its class-transition relevance" but no actual weight values are given [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the consumption computation formula fully specified? FR-001 references "net income (wage minus consumption)" but how consumption is derived from wage, MELT, and subsistence threshold is not defined [Gap, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK003 Are precaritization rate computation requirements documented? The spec defines the pathway (proletariat-to-lumpen) but does not specify the formula or inputs for computing this rate [Gap, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK004 Are stabilization rate computation requirements documented? The spec defines the pathway (lumpen-to-proletariat) but does not specify the formula or inputs for computing this rate [Gap, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK005 Is the accumulation threshold (wealth level required to transition from proletariat to LA) explicitly defined? Quickstart mentions "50th percentile threshold" but the spec does not specify this value or how it is determined [Gap, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK006 Are logging requirements for FR-011 specified with detail level, format, and destination? FR-011 says "log warnings for anomalous values" but does not define the logging mechanism or output [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "non-linearly" in FR-009 quantified? The spec states transition rates "increase non-linearly during crisis conditions" — research.md Section 5 specifies 2.5x/0.3x multipliers, but the spec itself leaves this ambiguous [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "each component weighted by its class-transition relevance" in FR-003 defined with specific weighting methodology? The data-model describes composite_risk but the weighting formula is not specified in the spec [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "approximately" in FR-001 savings rates (LA ~12%, proletariat ~3%) sufficiently precise for implementation? Research.md provides exact values but the spec uses approximate language [Clarity, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the scope of "consumption subsidy" in FR-008 clearly defined? The imperial rent adjustment formula appears only in research.md Section 4, not in the spec itself [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "stable" in SC-001 ("total share change less than 2% per period") defined as the sum of absolute changes across all classes or the maximum single-class change? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-001]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Are the transition flow equations in data-model.md consistent with the four pathways defined in FR-002? The equations show dispossession, accumulation, precaritization, and stabilization — do they correctly map to all FR-006 mechanism distinctions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002 vs data-model.md §State Transitions]
- [ ] CHK013 Does the quickstart.md API usage match the data-model.md entity definitions? Specifically, does `DefaultClassTransitionEngine` constructor signature match the dependency list in data-model.md? [Consistency, quickstart.md vs data-model.md]
- [ ] CHK014 Are the validation ranges in research.md Section 7 consistent with the success criteria ranges in SC-008? The class share validation Warning range for LA ([0.20, 0.60]) encompasses SC-008's expected range ([0.30, 0.50]) [Consistency, research.md §7 vs Spec §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the year range constraint consistent across all entities? Types define ge=2007, le=2030; hardcoded data covers 2007-2020; spec assumes "at minimum 2007-2020" — are requirements for years 2021-2030 (no hardcoded data) specified? [Consistency, data-model.md vs research.md §3]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Is SC-003 ("positive correlation") measurable without multi-county data? The MVP uses national hardcoded averages (same rate for all counties), making cross-county correlation impossible to test [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK017 Are the acceptance scenarios in US1 sufficiently precise? Scenario 1 says "approximately $1,500" — is the expected tolerance defined for acceptance testing? [Measurability, Spec §US1 Scenario 1]
- [ ] CHK018 Is SC-002 ("at least 2x the transition magnitude") defined with a specific metric? Is "transition magnitude" the sum of all rates, maximum single rate, or total share movement? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK019 Is SC-004 ("gradual upward mobility") quantified? What rate of decrease in lumpen share qualifies as "gradual" vs "rapid"? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are requirements defined for years outside the hardcoded data range (2021-2030)? EconomicConditions allows year up to 2030, but HardcodedNationalDispossessionSource only has 2007-2020 data — is the expected behavior specified? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements defined for the transition from crisis=True to crisis=False (recovery onset)? US4 covers crisis amplification but the spec does not address the transition dynamics when crisis flag changes between periods [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for multi-period simulation orchestration? US3 simulates one period; US5 requires multi-period — but no requirement specifies how periods are chained or whether intermediate distributions need validation [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements specified for when EconomicConditions.fips does not match ClassDistribution.fips? The spec does not define behavior for mismatched county identifiers [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK024 Are requirements defined for the scenario where all three dynamic classes converge to a single class (e.g., proletariat_share approaches 1.0)? Edge cases mention "all shares zero except one" but not convergence dynamics [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Mathematical Specification Quality

- [ ] CHK025 Is the normalization algorithm in research.md Section 6 fully specified? Steps mention "clamp each share to [0.0, 1.0]" then "normalize" — but clamping before normalizing can mask errors vs normalizing first then clamping [Clarity, research.md §6]
- [ ] CHK026 Are the transition flow equations numerically stable when class shares approach zero? Division by zero is possible if normalization sum approaches zero (all shares clamped to 0) [Edge Case, data-model.md §State Transitions]
- [ ] CHK027 Is the phi_adjustment formula `min(phi_hour * 2080 / wage, 0.05)` guarded against division by zero when wage=0? The edge case section mentions zero wage but the formula guard is not specified in requirements [Edge Case, research.md §4]
- [ ] CHK028 Are the hardcoded dispossession rates specified as annual probabilities or annual rates? The distinction matters for multi-period compounding — research.md labels them "rates" but uses them as probabilities [Clarity, research.md §3]

## Dependency & Integration Requirements

- [ ] CHK029 Are the exact imports from Feature 013 (ClassPosition, NoDataSentinel, NationalParameters) specified with module paths? Plan.md references them but spec.md describes the dependency generically [Completeness, Spec §Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK030 Is the integration point with Feature 014 (Throughput Position) specified beyond the assumption? The spec assumes it exists but no functional requirement references it directly [Gap, Spec §Assumptions vs §FR-*]
- [ ] CHK031 Is the integration point with Feature 015 (Gamma Visibility Tensor) specified beyond the assumption? The spec assumes it exists but no functional requirement references how gamma feeds into class dynamics [Gap, Spec §Assumptions vs §FR-*]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline after each item
- Items marked `[Gap]` indicate missing requirements that should be addressed before implementation
- Items marked `[Ambiguity]` indicate requirements needing clarification
- Items marked `[Consistency]` indicate potential conflicts between artifacts
- Reference links: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [research.md](../research.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md) | [quickstart.md](../quickstart.md)
